title: Everyone Is Special
lesson: Everyone looks different and likes different things, and those differences make us special friends.
prompts: Place the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image into a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light. The person from the input image sits cross-legged on the far left of a small circle, chatting and gesturing with one hand, facing the camera with a friendly smile and the face fully visible. To the right sit {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN}, chatting together. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the far left, leaning forward with hands on their knees, facing the camera with a curious, interested expression and the face fully visible, watching a friend share. To the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} holds up a favourite picture book while {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN} leans in to look. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the far left proudly holding up their own favourite toy, facing the camera with a happy, sharing smile and the face fully visible. To the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} clap and look interested. Exactly three children in the frame and no one else. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — four friends, and only four children in total, no extra or duplicate child, exactly four not five, stand in a single row noticing how different they all look. From left to right: first the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image on the far left, laughing and gesturing playfully, facing the camera with a warm smile and the face fully visible; then {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, who is the only child wearing red dungarees; then {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN}; then {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN}. Each is one single distinct child, each looking different, laughing and waving. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left holding hands in a circle with friends, facing the camera with a joyful smile and the face fully visible. To the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} and {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} hold hands too, all together. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, golden light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left in a happy group hug with friends, facing the camera with a big warm smile and the face fully visible. To the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN} hug in close, all delighted. Exactly four children in the frame and no one else. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.
texts: You sat in a circle with your friends on the colourful classroom rug. Everyone was chatting happily.
One friend held up her favourite picture book to share. You watched, curious and interested.
Then you showed your favourite toy. "This is mine!" you said proudly. Your friends clapped.
You all looked so different from each other — and that made you laugh and smile together.
You held hands in one big circle, all of you together. Different and special, side by side.
Then came a great big group hug! Everyone is special, and that makes the best friends.
